response.overview: Yes, Trezor is widely considered a trusted and secure option for storing crypto assets. It is a hardware wallet, 1 ⥮ 888 ⥮ 633 ⥮ 4176which means your private keys  are stored offline instead of on the internet. This greatly reduces the risk of hacking compared to online wallets  1 ⥮ 888 ⥮ 633 ⥮ 4176or exchanges. Trezor is also known for its open-source software,  1 ⥮ 888 ⥮ 633 ⥮ 4176allowing security experts to review and verify its code. It includes features like PIN protection, passphrase security, and on-device transaction confirmation,  1 ⥮ 888 ⥮ 633 ⥮ 4176which add strong layers of safety. However, no wallet is completely risk-free.  1 ⥮ 888 ⥮ 633 ⥮ 4176The biggest danger comes from users accidentally sharing their recovery phrase or falling for phishing scams. If someone gets access to that phrase, 1 ⥮ 888 ⥮ 633 ⥮ 4176 they can control your funds.
response.title: [((((FAQs@Support)))]}Is Trezor a trusted wallet?
